description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant with a cylindrical body and a domed top, featuring a rugged, weathered surface with visible patches of paint wear and rust. The hydrant has three prominent hose connection outlets, each capped and secured with metal chains, and a central operating nut on the top. The base is wide and bolted, designed for stability when mounted on pavement or concrete. The metal chains are attached to the side caps, preventing loss when the caps are removed. The surface is predominantly red with areas of blackened grime and some exposed metal, indicating long-term outdoor exposure. No manufacturer or model markings are visible on the exterior.
description: A weathered red fire hydrant with three hose outlets, chained caps, and a domed top, showing signs of rust and paint wear.
description_view_0: The hydrant is seen from an angle showing one side outlet with a chained cap and the front outlet, both clearly visible.
description_view_1: A direct front view highlights the central outlet and both side outlets, with all three chained caps visible.
description_view_2: This view emphasizes the opposite side outlet and the front outlet, with the chains and cap details prominent.
description_view_3: The rear view shows both side outlets and their chains, with the front outlet mostly obscured.
